skill.name: user-guide-writing
skill.description: Write clear and helpful user guides and tutorials for end users. Use when creating onboarding docs, how-to guides, or FAQ pages. Handles user-focused documentation, screenshots, step-by-step instructions.
skill.summary: Write clear and helpful user guides and tutorials for end users
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and network patterns, but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The skill provides documentation templates in markdown code blocks showing example URLs. No executable code, no network requests, no security risks. Pure documentation writing assistant.

content.user_title: Write User Guides That Actually Help People
content.value_statement: Users abandon products when documentation is confusing. This skill helps you create clear, step-by-step guides with screenshots, FAQs, and tutorials that reduce support tickets.
actual_capabilities: Write step-by-step quick start guides with numbered instructions
Create how-to tutorials organized by user task
Build FAQ sections with categorized questions and answers
Produce learning-focused tutorials with time estimates and prerequisites
Structure documentation with proper folder layouts and file organization
Write in user-friendly language avoiding technical jargon
limitations: Cannot access your actual product UI to take real screenshots
Does not verify that instructions match your actual product
Cannot test or validate that the generated docs work
Requires you to provide product-specific details and URLs

use_cases.title: Onboarding Documentation for SaaS Products
use_cases.description: Create step-by-step getting started guides that help new users sign up, configure their account, and complete their first key task within the first session.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and technical writers at SaaS companies

use_cases.title: Feature Release Documentation
use_cases.description: Write clear announcements and guides for new features, explaining what changed, why it matters, and how to use it with actionable steps.
use_cases.target_user: Developer relations and product teams launching new features

use_cases.title: Self-Service Support Articles
use_cases.description: Build a comprehensive FAQ and knowledge base that answers common questions, reducing support ticket volume and empowering users to solve problems independently.
use_cases.target_user: Customer success teams and support organizations

prompt_templates.title: Quick Start Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a quick start guide for [PRODUCT NAME] that helps new users get started in 5 minutes. Include 3-5 clear steps with placeholders for screenshots. The guide should cover: account creation, basic setup, and completing the first key action.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to create a basic onboarding document for new users

prompt_templates.title: How-To Task Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a how-to guide explaining how to [COMPLETE SPECIFIC TASK]. Include: a brief intro explaining what the user will accomplish, a prerequisites section, numbered step-by-step instructions, troubleshooting tips for common problems, and next steps for related tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need detailed task-focused documentation for specific features

prompt_templates.title: FAQ Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an FAQ section for [PRODUCT OR FEATURE]. Organize questions into 2-3 logical categories such as Account & Billing, Features, and Technical. Include 6-10 common questions with clear, concise answers. Use a friendly, helpful tone.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to build a self-service knowledge base

prompt_templates.title: Tutorial with Learning Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a tutorial titled '[TUTORIAL NAME]' that teaches users how to build something or accomplish a goal. Include: time estimate, difficulty level, prerequisites, step-by-step instructions with screenshots, pro tips, and next steps. Make it engaging and actionable.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to create in-depth learning content

output_examples.input: Write a quick start guide for a project management app
output_examples.output:
# Getting Started with ProjectPro

Welcome to ProjectPro! This guide will help you get started in 5 minutes.

## Step 1: Create Your Account

1. Go to [https://app.projectpro.io/signup]
2. Enter your email and create a password
3. Click Sign Up
4. Check your email and verify

## Step 2: Create Your First Project

1. Click + New Project
2. Enter a project name
3. Click Create

## Step 3: Add Your First Task

1. Click into your project
2. Click + Add Task
3. Enter task details
4. Click Save

You're ready to start managing projects!

output_examples.input: Create an FAQ about account billing
output_examples.output:
## Account & Billing

### How do I change my plan?

1. Go to Settings > Billing
2. Click Change Plan
3. Select your new plan
4. Confirm the change

Your new plan activates immediately.

### Can I get a refund?

We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee for annual plans. Contact support@projectpro.io within 30 days of purchase.

best_practices: Start with the user's goal, not your product features - focus on what users accomplish, not what the software does
Use visual anchors like screenshots, numbered steps, and bold text to break up content and guide the eye
Test your instructions by having someone unfamiliar with the product follow them - if they get stuck, add more detail
anti_patterns: Using technical jargon without explanation - avoid terms like API, endpoint, or backend unless you define them
Writing long paragraphs - keep sentences short and use bullet points for multiple related items
Assuming users know what comes next - explicitly state every click, selection, and action required

faq.question: What types of documentation can this skill create?
faq.answer: This skill can create quick start guides, how-to tutorials, FAQ sections, and learning-focused tutorials with step-by-step instructions.

faq.question: Does this skill generate actual screenshots?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates documentation with placeholders for screenshots. You will need to add your own screenshots to the final document.

faq.question: Can I use this for API documentation?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on user-facing documentation, not developer API docs. For API documentation, consider using a dedicated API documentation skill.

faq.question: How do I customize the output for my specific product?
faq.answer: Include your product name, specific features, actual URLs, and branding details in your prompt. The skill uses placeholders you can fill in.

faq.question: What tone should I use in user guides?
faq.answer: Use friendly, helpful, and clear language. Write from the user's perspective. Avoid condescension but treat users as intelligent newcomers.

faq.question: How often should I update my user guides?
faq.answer: Update guides whenever you release new features, change your UI, or notice recurring support questions that could be answered by documentation.
